publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Context managers; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Context managers: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? with is only shorthand for close at the final line and every __exit__ return value re-raises exceptions.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use with to guarantee protocol-driven cleanup during all exits, and return false from __exit__ unless the manager deliberately and safely suppresses the exception.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Use with to guarantee protocol-driven cleanup during all exits, and return false from __exit__ unless the manager deliberately and safely suppresses the exception.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Use with to guarantee protocol-driven cleanup during all exits, and return false from __exit__ unless the manager deliberately and safely suppresses the exception.
with is only shorthand for close at the final line and every __exit__ return value re-raises exceptions.

items.title: Context managers: canonical checkpoint

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'protocol-driven cleanup'. Use with to guarantee protocol-driven cleanup during all exits, and return false from __exit__ unless the manager deliberately and safely suppresses the exception.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Use with to guarantee protocol-driven cleanup during all exits, and return false from __exit__ unless the manager deliberately and safely suppresses the exception.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.
